generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitors physical infrastructure states for occupancy detection within Building Control Systems (BCS), integrated via BIM environment by Building Control Engineers. Supports real-time synchronization with Digital Twin through automated extraction from BIM models."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Deploys to monitor environmental parameters (e.g., temperature, humidity) within physical buildings, transmitting data to Digital Twin for real-time synchronization. Configured via Building Control Engineers within BIM environment."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalDataTransmitted
]

instance EquipmentStatusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Tracks operational status of building infrastructure components (e.g., HVAC, lighting systems) and transmits real-time data to Digital Twin for synchronization. Automatically extracted from BIM models via model-driven deployment framework."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EquipmentStatusDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Transmits occupancy detection data from physical sensors to Digital Twin via standardized protocols, enabling real-time synchronization."

]

instance EnvironmentalDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Carries environmental parameter data (e.g., temperature, humidity) from physical sensors to Digital Twin for continuous bidirectional synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalConditionSensor
]

instance EquipmentStatusDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Relays equipment operational status data from physical infrastructure to Digital Twin, supporting real-time monitoring and control."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EquipmentStatusSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BimEnrichedModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantically enriched BIM model containing device-specific metadata (e.g., sensor/actuator properties, control logic) serving as unified semantic foundation for Digital Twin transformation."

]

instance RealTimeDataSchema : DTDFVocab:Model [

    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler
]

instance OccupancyData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time occupancy detection data processed from physical sensors, used for monitoring and control services."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RealTimeDataExchangeEnabler
]

instance EnvironmentalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed environmental parameter data (e.g., temperature, humidity) enabling real-time monitoring and operational decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnvironmentalDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RealTimeDataExchangeEnabler
]

instance EquipmentStatusData : DTDFVocab:Data [

]

// C11: Enablers
instance ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Implements Model-Driven Engineering principles to extract structured representations from BIM models, automating generation of deployable Digital Twin artifacts (e.g., simulation models, configuration files)."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeviceModelingService, RealTimeMonitoringService, ControlAutomationService, CustomServiceDevelopmentService
]

instance RealTimeDataExchangeEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enables continuous bidirectional data exchange between physical buildings and Digital Twin via standardized protocols, ensuring operational parity."

]

instance InteractiveVisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Provides immersive interfaces (e.g., 3D BIM overlays, dashboards) for real-time monitoring and control, tailored to Facility Managers."

]

instance ModularFrameworkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control flows from the Digital Twin (DT) to physical infrastructure are mediated through **Facility Managers**, who interact with the DT to operate physical systems via **Building Control Systems (BCS)**. The framework enables **control signals** to be fed back to the BCS, though specific command types, message formats, or validation mechanisms are not detailed. **Feedback systems** are implied through bidirectional communication, but no explicit conflict resolution or command execution confirmation protocols are described. The document references **lighting systems** in related projects (e.g., TwinLight, BOREALIS) as an example of controlled physical assets, but no broader device-specific details (e.g., HVAC, structural actuators) are provided."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system in the AECO domain emphasizes **real-time synchronization** between physical assets (e.g., buildings) and their virtual representations to enable **live monitoring, simulation, and predictive analytics**. The architecture supports **real-time data exchange** to facilitate dynamic decision-making, though specific synchronization frequencies (e.g., sub-second, millisecond-level) or granularity requirements are not detailed. The system is designed to handle **temporal alignment** of static models (e.g., BIM) with runtime data streams, ensuring low-latency updates for operational optimization. The absence of explicit latency constraints suggests adaptability to varying time-sensitive applications, but the core requirement is **near-real-time synchronization** to maintain fidelity between physical and digital states."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** in the AECO domain is characterized by an **orchestration and coordination framework** designed to integrate **static models** (e.g., BIM—Building Information Modeling) with **runtime data** for real-time simulation, analysis, and optimization. The architecture must support **component integration** across **heterogeneous infrastructure systems**, requiring **resource management** to handle data-driven workflows (e.g., predictive analytics, decision-making). While no specific architecture patterns (e.g., microservices, event-driven) or technologies (e.g., IoT gateways, edge computing) are mentioned, the document emphasizes the need for **system-wide coordination mechanisms** to overcome the **fragmented nature of models and data**. The **TwinLight** and **BOREALIS** projects (funded by FFG) likely explore such orchestration, though their technical specifics are not provided in the excerpt."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans **five core phases**, each addressing the gap between static Building Information Modeling (BIM) representations and dynamic DT requirements. **1. Device Modeling**: Building Control Engineers define sensors, actuators, and controllers within the BIM environment, enriching it with semantic metadata (e.g., functional properties, control logic) to create a unified semantic foundation. **2. Model Transformation**: Leveraging **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE)** principles, the framework extracts structured representations from enriched BIM models via automated transformation pipelines, generating deployable DT artifacts (e.g., simulation models, real-time data schemas) with minimal manual intervention. **3. DT Deployment**: The framework supports **automated deployment** of DT artifacts, including integration with physical Building Control Systems (BCS) and initialization of real-time synchronization protocols. **4. Operational Monitoring**: Facility Managers interact with the DT through **immersive visualization interfaces**, enabling real-time monitoring of system states (e.g., sensor data, actuator statuses) and operational control via the twin. **5. Custom Service Extension**: DT Engineers extend functionality by integrating **custom services and plugins** through standardized extension points, allowing the DT to evolve with operational needs. **Transition Management** is governed by **modular architecture** and **standardized interfaces**, ensuring seamless handoffs between phases (e.g., from device modeling to deployment). **Lifecycle Governance** is implicit in the framework’s adherence to **use-case-driven requirements** (cf. Table II) and **stakeholder-specific roles** (cf. Table I), with explicit support for the entire DT life cycle from initial modeling to runtime evolution."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework addresses **fidelity requirements** by bridging the gap between **static BIM representations** and **dynamic DT operations**, ensuring that models accurately reflect the physical building’s state and behavior. **Validation and verification** are implicitly supported through the **model-driven deployment framework**, which enforces structured representations of BCS components (e.g., sensors, actuators, controllers) derived from BIM models. **Uncertainty sources** may arise from discrepancies between BIM’s static geometry and the real-time operational data, necessitating **automated synchronization mechanisms** to maintain model validity. While explicit **error detection and correction mechanisms** are not detailed, the framework’s reliance on **real-time data exchange** and **immersive visualization** suggests continuous monitoring and adjustment of model fidelity. **Quality assurance** is embedded in the **modular architecture**, where standardized extension points allow for integration of custom services, enabling stakeholders (e.g., Facility Managers) to validate and refine DT accuracy throughout its life cycle."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT framework employs a **modular, service-oriented architecture** with **standardized extension points**, enabling seamless integration of custom services and analytics. **Eclipse Hono** and **Ditto** are explicitly integrated for **real-time data exchange**, ensuring interoperability across heterogeneous systems. While the document does not specify network topology, bandwidth, latency, or redundancy mechanisms, it highlights **interoperability, scalability, and real-time capabilities** as critical non-functional requirements. Security protocols for network communication are not detailed, nor are explicit reliability or redundancy mechanisms described beyond the framework’s adaptability via runtime configuration."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) framework is deployed as a **centralized hub** within a **modular architecture**, supporting the life cycle management of building infrastructure. Deployment is initiated by **Building Control Engineers** using BIM tools for device modeling, followed by **Facility Managers** interacting with the operational DT for real-time monitoring and control. **DT Engineers** extend the framework’s capabilities via service integration at standardized extension points. The framework prioritizes **low-overhead maintainability**, enabling updates without full redeployment, and supports **scalability** from individual components to entire infrastructure systems. While hosting platforms (cloud/edge/on-premise) are not explicitly specified, the **runtime configuration management** and **modular design** imply flexibility in deployment models. Availability and reliability are assured through the framework’s **systematic requirements-driven approach**, addressing interoperability, scalability, and accessibility for users with varying technical expertise."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system requires integration with external systems to overcome the fragmented nature of data sources in AECO environments. This includes the fusion of static models (e.g., Building Information Models) with dynamic runtime data from IoT sensor networks and other operational systems. The document highlights the use of Eclipse Hono and Ditto as enablers for real-time data exchange, though it does not specify interoperability standards (e.g., IFC, OPC UA) or dependencies on external enterprise systems (e.g., ERP, CMMS). Integration patterns are not detailed beyond the need for standardized frameworks to bridge disparate data sources, ensuring seamless collaboration across the building lifecycle. The system’s horizontal integration is implicitly tied to its ability to process and analyze fragmented data streams in real-time, but no concrete protocols or external system dependencies are explicitly mentioned."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework addresses **standardization challenges** arising from BIM’s lack of seamless integration capabilities for Digital Twins. Key efforts include: **(1) Standardized Extension Points** within the modular architecture to ensure interoperability of custom services and analytics; **(2) Semantic Standardization** of BIM models to represent BCS components (e.g., sensors, actuators) uniformly; and **(3) Compliance with Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles** to automate transformations and reduce manual discrepancies. However, the document explicitly acknowledges the **absence of standardized integration frameworks** for DTs, highlighting a gap in industry-wide adoption. No specific industry standards (e.g., ISO, OMG, IFC) or certification requirements are mentioned, though the framework’s modular design implicitly aligns with interoperability goals."
]
